title: F1MATHS: Key numbers, stats and facts ahead of the Miami Grand Prix (F1Technical)
content: Season 2026 technical analysis — F1MATHS: Key numbers, stats and facts ahead of the Miami Grand Prix. The Miami International Autodrome's 5.412 km layout presents three aerodynamically distinct sectors, with Straight Mode deployment zones activated at 165 metres after Turn 18, 90 metres after Turn 16, and at the Turn 19 exit — reference points that will directly test 2026 power unit energy deployment behaviour under revised mid-season regulations. The official speed trap 150 metres before Turn 17 will serve as a primary power unit benchmark across the field. Historically low tyre degradation supports a one-stop strategy, with Oscar Piastri's 2025 McLaren victory built on a C4 Medium-to-C3 Hard switch at lap 29 of 57. Max Verstappen holds the outright lap record at 1:29.708, while Lando Norris set the 2025 fastest race lap at 1:29.746 against Verstappen's pole of 1:26.204, a 2.5-second qualifying-to-race pace delta indicating significant fuel and tyre management trade-offs relevant to 2026 setup calibration.

content: Season 2026 technical analysis — F1 ANALYSIS: Mapping Formula One’s Long‑Term Circuit Contracts. title: Chinese Grand Prix 2026 — Sprint Race
content:
Season 2026, Round 2 Sprint: Chinese Grand Prix. George Russell took sprint qualifying pole at Shanghai International Circuit for the 2026 Chinese Grand Prix, with Andrea Kimi Antonelli completing an all-Mercedes front row. Lando Norris qualified third for McLaren, ahead of Lewis Hamilton and Oscar Piastri.

Russell converted pole into sprint race victory, holding position throughout. Charles Leclerc was the most notable finisher relative to grid position among the frontrunners, advancing four places from sixth to claim second for Ferrari. Lewis Hamilton completed the Ferrari podium in third, gaining one place from fourth. Andrea Kimi Antonelli dropped three positions to fifth despite starting second, suggesting early race difficulties for the Mercedes junior. Liam Lawson recorded the largest net gain among classified finishers, rising six places from thirteenth to seventh for RB F1 Team, mirrored by Alexander Albon's six-place advance from twenty-second. Pierre Gasly fell four places to eleventh, while Isack Hadjar dropped five to fifteenth. Nico Hülkenberg retired from eleventh, losing nine positions. Ferrari's strong race pace relative to single-lap performance and Mercedes' tyre management advantage from the front suggest both constructors merit close attention for the Grand Prix proper.

title: Miami Grand Prix 2026 — Sprint Race
content: Season 2026, Round 4 Sprint: Miami Grand Prix. Lando Norris claimed sprint qualifying pole at Miami International Autodrome, with Andrea Kimi Antonelli of Mercedes lining up second and Oscar Piastri completing an all-McLaren second row alongside Charles Leclerc of Ferrari in fourth. Norris converted pole into a sprint race victory, leading throughout, while Piastri advanced one position to second, completing a McLaren one-two. Leclerc held third to give Ferrari a podium finish. George Russell was the most notable forward mover among the frontrunners, gaining two places to finish fourth, while Antonelli dropped four positions from second to sixth, suggesting difficulties in race trim relative to qualifying pace. Esteban Ocon and Fernando Alonso each gained five places from deep in the field. Gabriel Bortoleto of Audi was disqualified, and Nico Hülkenberg did not start. McLaren's dominant pace across both sprint sessions indicates strong race-day potential, while Mercedes may face tyre management considerations over the longer grand prix distance.

title: Canadian Grand Prix 2026 — Sprint Race
content:
Season 2026, Round 5 Sprint: Canadian Grand Prix. George Russell took sprint qualifying pole at Circuit Gilles Villeneuve for the 2026 Canadian Grand Prix, with Andrea Kimi Antonelli completing an all-Mercedes front row. Lando Norris qualified third for McLaren, ahead of teammate Oscar Piastri in fourth, while Lewis Hamilton and Charles Leclerc filled the fifth and sixth positions for Ferrari.

Russell converted pole into sprint race victory, holding position throughout. Norris advanced one place from third to second, with Antonelli dropping to third after failing to defend second. Piastri maintained fourth. The most significant positive movers were Liam Lawson, who gained seven places from eighteenth to eleventh, and Franco Colapinto, who rose four positions from thirteenth to ninth. Isack Hadjar was the sharpest faller, dropping thirteen places to twenty-first, while Fernando Alonso retired from fifteenth. Nico Hülkenberg also lost four positions.

The sprint outcome indicates Mercedes holds a clear single-lap and race-pace advantage at this circuit, with McLaren competitive over distance. Ferrari's relative positional stagnation and Red Bull's mid-field drift from Hadjar suggest tyre management on the abrasive Montreal surface may present challenges for those teams across the longer grand prix distance.

title: Monaco Grand Prix 2026 — Qualifying
content: Season 2026, Round 6 Qualifying: Monaco Grand Prix. Andrea Kimi Antonelli claimed pole position for the Monaco Grand Prix driving for Mercedes, setting a Q3 best of 1:12.051. Max Verstappen qualified second for Red Bull just 0.043 seconds behind, representing an exceptionally tight margin at the front. Lewis Hamilton placed third for Ferrari at 1:12.279, with teammate Charles Leclerc fourth at 1:12.351, giving Ferrari a strong combined presence on the second row. Isack Hadjar rounded out the top five for Red Bull at 1:12.434, a notably strong performance from the sophomore driver. George Russell qualified sixth for Mercedes, with both McLaren drivers Oscar Piastri and Lando Norris in seventh and eighth, suggesting McLaren lacked their typical single-lap competitiveness at this circuit. Pierre Gasly delivered a solid ninth for Alpine F1 Team, while Liam Lawson took tenth for RB F1 Team. Fernando Alonso and Lance Stroll were eliminated in Q1 in 21st and 22nd respectively, a significant underperformance for Aston Martin. The compactness of the top five — covered by just 0.383 seconds — indicates closely matched race pace among the leading teams, though Monaco's limited overtaking opportunities will make grid position a decisive factor in Sunday's race outcome.

title: Monaco Grand Prix 2026 — Race Result & Analysis
content:
Season 2026, Round 6: Monaco Grand Prix. Andrea Kimi Antonelli took victory at the Monaco Grand Prix for Mercedes, completing the race distance in 2 hours 23 minutes 31.243 seconds. Lewis Hamilton finished second for Ferrari, 6.271 seconds adrift, with Isack Hadjar claiming third for Red Bull a further 17.123 seconds behind. Oscar Piastri placed fourth for McLaren, followed by Liam Lawson and Arvid Lindblad, both representing RB F1 Team, in fifth and sixth respectively. Pierre Gasly took seventh for Alpine F1 Team, Alexander Albon eighth for Williams, Esteban Ocon ninth for Haas F1 Team, and Sergio Pérez tenth for Cadillac F1 Team to complete the points positions. Grid position data was unavailable for this round, preventing position-change analysis across the field.

Antonelli also took pole position with a lap of 1:12.051, converting front-row advantage into the race win. No fastest lap data was available for this event. Pit stop data was not recorded, making strategy comparison between teams impossible for this round.

Seven drivers retired. Max Verstappen for Red Bull did not start the race, retiring on lap zero. Valtteri Bottas of Cadillac F1 Team retired on lap 15, Oliver Bearman of Haas F1 Team on lap 27, Lando Norris of McLaren on lap 43, Lance Stroll of Aston Martin on lap 56, Charles Leclerc of Ferrari on lap 64, and Carlos Sainz of Williams on lap 70.

In the championship standings, Antonelli extended his lead to 156 points, gaining 25. Hamilton moved up two positions to second with 90 points, while George Russell dropped to third on 88. Leclerc fell to fourth having scored no points. Isack Hadjar made the largest standings gain, rising four places to eighth on 29 points.

title: Barcelona Grand Prix 2026 — Qualifying
content:
Season 2026, Round 7 Qualifying: Barcelona Grand Prix. George Russell claimed pole position for the Barcelona Grand Prix at Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya, setting a best lap of 1:14.679 for Mercedes. Lewis Hamilton qualified second for Ferrari, just 0.064 seconds adrift, marking a notable result for the former Mercedes driver at a circuit where he historically excelled. Andrea Kimi Antonelli completed the second Mercedes entry in third at 1:14.998, 0.319 seconds off pole. Lando Norris and Max Verstappen qualified fourth and fifth respectively, separated by only 0.020 seconds, with both drivers within 0.342 seconds of Russell.

Isack Hadjar impressed in sixth for Red Bull, outqualifying teammate Verstappen in Q2 pace comparisons and ahead of Oscar Piastri in seventh. Charles Leclerc's failure to set a Q3 lap time is a significant anomaly, leaving the Ferrari driver tenth on the grid despite his Q2 time of 1:15.281 being competitive. Aston Martin struggled severely, with both Fernando Alonso and Lance Stroll eliminated in Q1 and filling the final two grid positions.

The qualifying order indicates Mercedes holds a clear performance advantage at this circuit, with Ferrari and McLaren closely matched in second-tier pace. Red Bull's presence in the top five suggests competitive race pace, though the gap to Mercedes may be difficult to bridge over a full race distance.

title: Barcelona Grand Prix 2026 — Race Result & Analysis
content:
Season 2026, Round 7: Barcelona Grand Prix. Lewis Hamilton won the 2026 Barcelona Grand Prix for Ferrari, starting from second on the grid and finishing 19.561 seconds ahead of George Russell in the Mercedes. Lando Norris completed the podium for McLaren, a further 4.158 seconds back, having gained one position from fourth on the grid. Max Verstappen advanced one place from fifth to fourth, while Oscar Piastri moved from seventh to fifth for McLaren. The most notable gainers across the field were Pierre Gasly and Franco Colapinto, both of Alpine F1 Team, who climbed seven and five positions respectively to finish seventh and eighth. Andrea Kimi Antonelli, who started third for Mercedes, and Nico Hülkenberg of Audi suffered the sharpest position losses, dropping thirteen and eleven places respectively before retiring from the race.

George Russell took pole position with a time of 1:14.679 but was unable to convert it into victory, losing the lead to Hamilton during the race. Hamilton also set the fastest lap on lap 44 with a 1:20.122, contributing an additional championship point.

Pit stop strategy was predominantly one to two stops. Oscar Piastri recorded the event's fastest individual stop at 21.776 seconds on lap 14. Hamilton ran a two-stop strategy on laps 11 and 27, as did Verstappen on laps 12 and 29. Russell managed the race on a single stop at lap 12, averaging 22.08 seconds. No unusually slow stops were recorded in the data.

Notable retirements included Charles Leclerc of Ferrari on lap 62, Antonelli on lap 61, Nico Hülkenberg on lap 29, Valtteri Bottas on lap 15, and Lance Stroll on lap 5 — Stroll's early exit suggesting a first-lap or mechanical incident.

In the championship standings, Antonelli retained the lead with 156 points, but scored nothing after retiring, allowing Hamilton to close the gap significantly to 41 points by banking 25. George Russell moved to 106 points. Lando Norris overtook Oscar Piastri to take fifth in the standings, the two McLaren drivers separated by just five points.

title: Austrian Grand Prix 2026 — Qualifying
content: Season 2026, Round 8 Qualifying: Austrian Grand Prix. George Russell claimed pole position for the Austrian Grand Prix at the Red Bull Ring, setting a Q3 best of 1:06.113 for Mercedes. Charles Leclerc qualified second for Ferrari with a 1:06.349, a gap of 0.236 seconds to Russell. Lewis Hamilton completed a strong Ferrari showing in third with a 1:06.408, just 0.059 seconds behind his teammate. Andrea Kimi Antonelli placed fourth for Mercedes at 1:06.414, with Max Verstappen fifth for Red Bull Racing at 1:06.475, meaning the top five were covered by 0.362 seconds. Lando Norris and Oscar Piastri qualified sixth and seventh for McLaren, with both drivers within 0.4 seconds of pole. Isack Hadjar reached Q3 in eighth, a noteworthy result for the Red Bull junior driver. Both RB F1 Team cars advanced to Q3, with Liam Lawson ninth and Arvid Lindblad tenth. Aston Martin's Fernando Alonso and Lance Stroll were notably slow, lapping over three and four seconds off pole respectively, suggesting significant competitiveness concerns. The tight top-five spread across Mercedes, Ferrari, Red Bull, and McLaren indicates a closely contested race pace environment, with no single team holding a clear performance advantage heading into the grand prix.

title: Austrian Grand Prix 2026 — Race Result & Analysis
content:
Season 2026, Round 8: Austrian Grand Prix. George Russell converted pole position into victory at the 2026 Austrian Grand Prix, completing the race in 1:26:37.979 and holding off Max Verstappen by 1.611 seconds, with Andrea Kimi Antonelli a further 0.375 seconds back in third to complete a Mercedes one-three. Verstappen was the most significant forward mover among the frontrunners, advancing three places from fifth on the grid to second. Oscar Piastri similarly gained three positions to finish fourth, while Charles Leclerc suffered the sharpest regression of any points scorer, dropping six places from second on the grid to eighth. Lewis Hamilton fell two positions from third to fifth, and Carlos Sainz lost three places before retiring.

George Russell took pole with a 1:06.113, converting that advantage into a wire-to-wire win. Andrea Kimi Antonelli set the fastest lap on lap 59 with a 1:10.374, securing the bonus point for Mercedes. Ferrari recorded the single fastest individual pit stop of the race, Charles Leclerc's second stop on lap 37 clocking 21.020 seconds. Lewis Hamilton was the only front-runner to make three stops, pitting on laps 12, 25, and 42, which did not yield a net position gain. Esteban Ocon recorded a notably elevated average stop duration of 23.67 seconds across two stops, suggesting a procedural issue. Lance Stroll averaged 23.52 seconds for his sole stop before retiring on lap 45.

Valtteri Bottas and Sergio Pérez both retired early for Cadillac F1 Team, on laps 2 and 4 respectively, eliminating both entries before the race developed. Carlos Sainz retired from Williams on lap 23, and Lance Stroll retired for Aston Martin on lap 45.

In the championship, Andrea Kimi Antonelli remains first on 171 points. George Russell's 25-point haul moved him above Lewis Hamilton into second on 131 points, with Hamilton third on 125. Oscar Piastri climbed two positions to fourth on 80 points, drawing level with Lando Norris and pushing Charles Leclerc down two places to sixth, also on 79 points. Max Verstappen added 18 points to reach 73, remaining seventh.

title: British Grand Prix 2026 — Sprint Race
content: Season 2026, Round 9 Sprint: British Grand Prix. Lewis Hamilton took sprint qualifying pole at Silverstone Circuit for the 2026 British Grand Prix, with Andrea Kimi Antonelli of Mercedes completing the front row. Max Verstappen of Red Bull and Charles Leclerc of Ferrari occupied the second row. In the sprint race, Antonelli converted his front-row start into victory, with Hamilton finishing second and Lando Norris of McLaren completing the podium after advancing three places from sixth on the grid. George Russell gained one position to finish fourth, while Verstappen dropped three places from third to sixth, suggesting Red Bull faced pace or tyre management difficulties over the sprint distance. Alexander Albon of Williams was the largest net gainer, moving four positions forward from twenty-second to eighteenth, while Sergio Pérez of Cadillac F1 Team lost four places to finish last. The sprint results indicate Ferrari and Mercedes hold strong single-lap and race pace at Silverstone, with McLaren demonstrating competitive race-trim performance that could be significant over the longer Grand Prix distance and across tyre compounds.

title: British Grand Prix 2026 — Qualifying
content: Season 2026, Round 9 Qualifying: British Grand Prix. Andrea Kimi Antonelli took pole position for the British Grand Prix at Silverstone Circuit, setting a Q3 lap time of 1:28.111 for Mercedes. Charles Leclerc qualified second for Ferrari, 0.175 seconds adrift, with teammate Lewis Hamilton third at 1:28.458, giving Ferrari a strong front-row challenge. George Russell completed the second row for Mercedes at 1:28.481, just 0.023 seconds behind Hamilton, indicating an exceptionally tight midfield of the top four. Isack Hadjar was an impressive fifth for Red Bull at 1:28.746, outqualifying both McLaren drivers and veteran teammate Max Verstappen, who ended seventh at 1:28.893. Lando Norris in sixth and Oscar Piastri in eighth represent a subdued qualifying for McLaren relative to their recent form. Arvid Lindblad reached Q3 for RB F1 Team in ninth, a notable result for the junior outfit. Aston Martin's Lance Stroll and Fernando Alonso were eliminated in Q1 with times over 1.5 seconds off the pace, underscoring a significant competitiveness gap. The front-row lockout for Mercedes and Ferrari, combined with Red Bull's moderate pace, suggests a race likely to be contested between those two constructors, with Red Bull and McLaren potentially operating as secondary challengers.

title: British Grand Prix 2026 — Race Result & Analysis
content:
Season 2026, Round 9: British Grand Prix. Charles Leclerc won the 2026 British Grand Prix at Silverstone Circuit, crossing the line in 1:27:11.335 to claim 25 points for Ferrari. George Russell finished second for Mercedes, 0.427 seconds behind, while Lewis Hamilton completed a Ferrari one-two-three, a further 0.345 seconds back in third. Notable gainers across the field included Franco Colapinto, who advanced ten positions from nineteenth on the grid to ninth, and Liam Lawson, who rose four places from tenth to sixth for RB F1 Team. Gabriel Bortoleto gained three positions to eighth for Audi. The most significant decliner was Andrea Kimi Antonelli, who fell fifteen places from pole to sixteenth at the finish.

Andrea Kimi Antonelli took pole position for Mercedes with a lap of 1:28.111 and also set the fastest lap on lap 37 with a time of 1:31.777, though neither translated into a race result, with Antonelli ultimately classified sixteenth.

George Russell recorded the race's fastest pit stop at 28.337 seconds on lap 34. Most frontrunners completed single-stop strategies, including Leclerc on lap 25, Hamilton on lap 23, and Isack Hadjar on lap 19. Russell and Lando Norris each ran two stops. Esteban Ocon's single stop averaged 43.25 seconds, the slowest of any driver and suggestive of a procedural issue. Alexander Albon made four stops before retiring on lap 43.

Max Verstappen, Alexander Albon, and Nico Hülkenberg all retired. Verstappen's failure on lap 46 limited him to three bonus points, costing ground in the championship. In the standings, Charles Leclerc rose two positions to fourth after scoring 25 points, while Oscar Piastri dropped two places to sixth having scored only two points. Andrea Kimi Antonelli leads with 179 points, with George Russell closing to 154 and Lewis Hamilton at 147.

title: Belgian Grand Prix 2026 — Qualifying
content:
Season 2026, Round 10 Qualifying: Belgian Grand Prix. Andrea Kimi Antonelli claimed pole position for the Belgian Grand Prix at Circuit de Spa-Francorchamps, giving Mercedes the top qualifying spot. Max Verstappen qualified second for Red Bull, with Lando Norris third for McLaren. George Russell secured fourth for Mercedes, and Charles Leclerc took fifth for Ferrari. Lap time data is unavailable, so precise gaps between these positions cannot be determined.

Notable performances include Arvid Lindblad qualifying eighth for RB F1 Team and Gabriel Bortoleto ninth for Audi, both representing strong results for their respective teams against more established midfield rivals. Isack Hadjar rounded out the top ten for Red Bull. Oscar Piastri qualified seventh, placing him behind teammate Norris but also behind Lindblad, which stands out as a modest underperformance for McLaren at this circuit. Both Aston Martin drivers, Fernando Alonso and Lance Stroll, failed to progress beyond Q1, qualifying twenty-first and twenty-second respectively, representing a significant shortfall for that team.

The front-row lockout for Mercedes and Red Bull, combined with McLaren's split across positions three and seven, suggests Mercedes and Red Bull hold the strongest race pace credentials at Spa, with Ferrari and McLaren likely forming a secondary competitive group.

title: Belgian Grand Prix 2026 — Race Result & Analysis
content:
Season 2026, Round 10: Belgian Grand Prix. Andrea Kimi Antonelli converted pole position into victory at the 2026 Belgian Grand Prix, winning from the front in a total race time of 1:24:42.479 at Circuit de Spa-Francorchamps. Charles Leclerc finished second for Ferrari, 1.952 seconds adrift having advanced two places from his grid position of fourth, while Max Verstappen completed the podium for Red Bull, 11.586 seconds behind the leader after dropping one place from second on the grid. Lewis Hamilton gained one position to finish fourth, and Oscar Piastri similarly advanced one place to fifth. The standout mover across the full field was Isack Hadjar, who climbed fifteen places from twenty-first on the grid to sixth at the flag for Red Bull. Lando Norris also recovered strongly, gaining six positions from thirteenth to seventh.

Andrea Kimi Antonelli took pole with a time of 1:44.361, and Lando Norris set the fastest lap on lap 44 with a 1:48.890. The majority of the field operated a single-stop strategy, with Alexander Albon recording the fastest individual stop of 22.638 seconds on lap 18. Notably, Lewis Hamilton's stop averaged 32.2 seconds and Carlos Sainz's 34.84 seconds, suggesting possible issues during those service visits. Isack Hadjar made three stops, the only driver to do so.

George Russell retired from the race on lap 0, indicating a formation lap or start incident. Lance Stroll and Sergio Pérez also retired, on laps 25 and 13 respectively.

In the championship, Antonelli extended his lead to 204 points, now 45 ahead of Lewis Hamilton, who moved above George Russell into second after Russell's retirement yielded zero points.
